behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before any downstream processing; missing fields return one clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily orchestration and validation logic inside one process. No real I/O is required to catch the real failure (missing-field aggregation or accidental downstream invocation).
behaviors.test_description: Call intake validation/orchestrator with payloads missing multiple required fields (e.g., missing name and insurance ID). Assert error includes all missing fields in one response and assert downstream steps are not invoked.

behaviors.description: DOB validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Pure deterministic date-parsing and range-check logic; mocks do not hide key failures here.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test DOB validator with: future date, date older than 150 years, invalid string, and valid boundary dates. Assert exact rejection/acceptance behavior.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code to payer API and correctly interprets eligible/not eligible/pending responses.
behaviors.justification: Request-shape and response-mapping bugs are often hidden by in-process mocks. Real HTTP interaction is the failure boundary for payload serialization, headers, and status/body handling.
behaviors.test_description: Run the eligibility client against a real HTTP test server endpoint that records requests. Verify transmitted patient ID/plan code and verify mapping of three payer responses (eligible, not eligible, pending) into internal status.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is marked pending and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Network-unreachable behavior depends on real transport failures (connection refused/timeout), which mocks may not reproduce faithfully.
behaviors.test_description: Point payer API client to an unreachable endpoint (or force real timeout) and execute intake. Assert eligibility is set to pending and subsequent intake steps still execute.

behaviors.description: AI symptom triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output behavior, which is non-deterministic and cannot be validated by deterministic mocks alone.
behaviors.test_description: Submit emergency symptom combinations to the real triage model across repeated runs and assert high-urgency classification meets acceptance threshold (e.g., high urgency in >=X% of runs).

behaviors.description: Symptom triage returns a clinician-reviewable natural language explanation, not only a numeric score.
behaviors.justification: The requirement is about generated language quality/presence from the AI engine, which mocks can trivially satisfy and hide real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call real triage model with representative cases and assert output contains both urgency value and non-trivial explanatory text meeting minimal rubric (medical rationale elements, not empty/template-only).

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (high: same-day, medium: within 48h).
behaviors.justification: This depends on scheduler logic plus shared calendar DB state; pure unit mocks can miss query/order and persistence interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Seed calendar DB with realistic slot availability, run scheduling for high and medium urgency cases, and assert selected slot is first eligible and satisfies timing windows.

behaviors.description: Scheduling avoids double-booking when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Race-condition failures emerge from real concurrent access to shared DB constraints/transactions; in-process mocks can miss this.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two concurrent scheduling attempts targeting overlapping availability against the real calendar DB schema/constraints. Assert only one appointment claims a given slot and the other is reassigned or fails safely.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Message composition is mostly deterministic formatting/template logic. Real infra is not required to catch omission/mapping bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation builder with representative appointment/visit-type inputs and assert rendered message includes all required fields and correct prep instructions for each visit type.

behaviors.description: Full intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes automatically for a real submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end multi-step pipeline that includes an agentic triage step plus non-agentic external dependencies; only workflow-level testing catches cross-step handoff failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real end-to-end intake submission through all stages with real integrations enabled (payer path, triage model, scheduling DB, confirmation dispatch stub/collector). Assert successful completion and coherent outputs across stage boundaries.
